hook: A mutable learned index can lose its query guarantees under deletions: prior dynamic PGM-style approaches can degrade to worst-case range-query time O(N + Σ_i(ε + log |h_i|)), while the 2025 dynamic learned-index result keeps worst-case updates at O(log^2 n + ε). At the same time, write-local workloads make coarse locking collapse, which is why the proposed design pairs deletion-safe indexing with delta chains and batch-parallel commits.
why_it_matters: If you are learning how to design mutable data structures and algorithms, this gives you a concrete target: separate read-optimized structure from update handling so deletions and hotspot writes do not dominate behavior.
action: Write a one-page design note with three labeled boxes—"base learned index," "per-segment delta chains," and "batch compaction"—and under each box list one invariant and one failure mode taken from the article.
